ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' — ISO/IMG-mounted LNK launches signed binary that side-loads adjacent DLL
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Lazarus / Labyrinth Chollima social-engineering chain (Mandiant/Microsoft tracked as the Diamond Sleet 'Dream Job' / 'North Star' lure): a ZIP→ISO/IMG/VHD mounted by the recipient, an LNK inside that launches a renamed legitimate signed Windows binary (e.g. CCleaner.exe, IExpress.exe, GoogleUpdate.exe, wsmprovhost.exe) which then side-loads a malicious DLL that it expects to find adjacent to it. The discriminator is process-from-mounted-volume + image-load-of-unsigned-DLL-from-the-same-non-C-drive within 60s.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as commandline values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" AND (Processes.process_path="D:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="E:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="F:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="G:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="H:\\*") AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as load_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND (Filesystem.file_path="D:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="E:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="F:\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="G:\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | where load_time>=firstTime AND load_time<=(firstTime+60) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' / Diamond Sleet ISO-LNK lure — process spawned by explorer.exe
// from a mounted ISO/IMG/VHD (drive letters D:..Z:) that loads an unsigned DLL adjacent to it.
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSeconds = 60;
let ProcsFromMountedVolume =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    | project ProcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProcId=ProcessId, BinFile=FileName, BinFolder=FolderPath,
              ProcessCommandLine, BinSha=SHA256;
let AdjacentDllLoads =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    | project LoadTime=Timestamp, DeviceId,
              LoaderProcId=InitiatingProcessId, LoaderName=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DllName=FileName, DllFolder=FolderPath, DllSha=SHA256;
ProcsFromMountedVolume
| join kind=inner AdjacentDllLoads on DeviceId, $left.ProcId == $right.LoaderProcId
| where LoadTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + WindowSeconds * 1s)
| where DllFolder =~ BinFolder              // DLL dropped right next to the legit-looking binary
| where DllName !startswith "api-ms-win" and DllName !startswith "vcruntime"
| project ProcTime, LoadTime, DeviceName, AccountName, BinFile, BinFolder,
          ProcessCommandLine, BinSha, DllName, DllSha
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Lazarus 'Operation Dream Job' — Sentinel port using SecurityEvent 4688 + Sysmon EID 7
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSeconds = 60s;
let MountedProc =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\explorer.exe"
    | where NewProcessName matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project ProcTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ProcId=NewProcessId, BinFile=NewProcessName, CommandLine;
let DllLoad =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             ProcessId = tostring(EventData.ProcessId),
             Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed)
    | where ImageLoaded matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    | where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
    | where Signed =~ "false"
    | project LoadTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcessId, Image, ImageLoaded;
MountedProc
| join kind=inner DllLoad on Computer, $left.ProcId == $right.ProcessId
| where LoadTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + WindowSeconds)
| project ProcTime, LoadTime, Computer, Account, BinFile, CommandLine, ImageLoaded
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus Operation Dream Job - Process Launch From Mounted ISO/IMG by Explorer
id: 7c2a9e1d-3f4b-4a8e-9b6c-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a process spawned by explorer.exe from a non-C: drive letter (D:..Z:),
  consistent with a user double-clicking an LNK inside a mounted ISO/IMG/VHD
  delivered via a fake job-description archive — the canonical initial-access
  pattern for Lazarus Group 'Operation Dream Job' (Diamond Sleet / Labyrinth Chollima).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/dream-jobs-suite-of-attack-tools
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.defense-evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
    Image|re: '(?i)^[D-Z]:\\.+\.exe$'
  filter_setup_media:
    Image|contains:
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\autorun.exe'
      - '\install.exe'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter_setup_media and not filter_machine_account
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - Hashes
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software install media that the user mounted from a recognised vendor
level: high
ucs.rationale: Lazarus has used the ISO/IMG/ZIP-bundled-LNK-then-side-loaded-DLL chain repeatedly across Operation Dream Job (2020–2024), the Polish/Czech defence campaigns (LightlessCan, ThreatNeedle, BLINDINGCAN), and the npm developer-targeting clusters tracked by Microsoft as Diamond Sleet. The combination — explorer.exe parent + non-C drive + adjacent unsigned DLL load within 60s — is virtually never produc

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus trojanized signed-software supply-chain side-load (3CX / X_TRADER / JumpCloud pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts the supply-chain tradecraft Mandiant/CrowdStrike attribute to UNC4736 / Labyrinth Chollima — a legitimately signed business application (3CXDesktopApp.exe, X_TRADER.exe, MagicLine, etc.) installed under Program Files loads a malicious adjacent DLL (e.g. ffmpeg.dll, d3dcompiler_47.dll, msvcr100.dll) which then beacons to attacker infrastructure within minutes. The first-time-seen DLL+parent pair plus rare external egress from a Program-Files-resident process is the discriminator.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest) as remote_ips values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_ports dc(All_Traffic.dest) as unique_dests min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app="*\\Program Files\\*" OR All_Traffic.app="*\\Program Files (x86)\\*") AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rex field=app "(?<vendor_dir>\\\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\\\[^\\\\]+)" | join type=outer src vendor_dir [| tstats summariesonly=true count as historical_count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" earliest=-60d@d latest=-7d@d by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rex field=app "(?<vendor_dir>\\\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\\\[^\\\\]+)" | stats sum(historical_count) as historical_count by src vendor_dir] | where isnull(historical_count) OR historical_count<5 | where unique_dests<=5 AND count<200 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Lazarus / Diamond Sleet supply-chain side-load (3CX, X_TRADER, JumpCloud, MagicLine pattern)
// Signed Program-Files binary loads a same-folder DLL that is brand-new for that parent
// AND that process initiates external HTTPS within 10 minutes — the 3CX kill-chain in one query.
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let BaselineWindow = 60d;
let ExpectedDlls = dynamic(["api-ms-win","vcruntime","mfc","msvcp","ucrtbase"]);
let HistoricDllForParent =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(BaselineWindow) .. ago(LookbackHours))
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Program Files\", @"\Program Files (x86)\")
    | summarize by ParentExe=tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName), DllName=tolower(FileName);
let SuspectLoads =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Program Files\", @"\Program Files (x86)\")
    | where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath          // DLL adjacent to the signed exe
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where not(FileName has_any (ExpectedDlls))
    | extend ParentExe=tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName), DllNameLow=tolower(FileName)
    | join kind=leftanti HistoricDllForParent on ParentExe, $left.DllNameLow == $right.DllName
    | project LoadTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              LoaderPid=InitiatingProcessId, ParentExe=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentSha=InitiatingProcessSHA256, DllName=FileName, DllSha=SHA256;
let ExternalEgress =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | summarize NetTime=min(Timestamp), Dests=make_set(RemoteUrl, 5),
                IPs=make_set(RemoteIP, 5), Ports=make_set(RemotePort, 5),
                ConnCount=count()
                by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName;
SuspectLoads
| join kind=inner ExternalEgress on DeviceId, $left.LoaderPid == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 10m)
| project LoadTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, LoadTime),
          DeviceName, ParentExe, ParentSha, DllName, DllSha,
          Dests, IPs, Ports, ConnCount
| order by LoadTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel port — Sysmon EID 7 (image load) joined with Sysmon EID 3 (network connect)
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let ExpectedDlls = dynamic(["api-ms-win","vcruntime","mfc","msvcp","ucrtbase"]);
let SuspectLoads =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
             ProcessGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
             ProcessId = tostring(EventData.ProcessId)
    | where Image has_any ("\\Program Files\\", "\\Program Files (x86)\\")
    | where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
    | where Signed =~ "false"
    | extend ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
             LoadedDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
    | where ImageDir =~ LoadedDir
    | where not(ImageLoaded has_any (ExpectedDlls))
    | project LoadTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcessGuid, ProcessId, Image, ImageLoaded;
let Egress =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 3
    | extend ProcessGuid = tostring(EventData.ProcessGuid),
             DestinationIp = tostring(EventData.DestinationIp),
             DestinationHostname = tostring(EventData.DestinationHostname),
             DestinationPort = toint(EventData.DestinationPort),
             Initiated = tostring(EventData.Initiated)
    | where Initiated =~ "true"
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DestinationIp))
    | where DestinationPort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | project NetTime=TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcessGuid,
              DestinationIp, DestinationHostname, DestinationPort;
SuspectLoads
| join kind=inner Egress on Computer, ProcessGuid
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 10m)
| summarize FirstLoad=min(LoadTime), FirstNet=min(NetTime),
            Dests=make_set(DestinationHostname, 10), IPs=make_set(DestinationIp, 10)
            by Computer, Image, ImageLoaded
| order by FirstLoad desc
ucs.rationale: This is the exact tradecraft Mandiant attributed to Lazarus / UNC4736 in the 3CXDesktopApp compromise (March 2023, ffmpeg.dll side-loaded by the signed Electron exe, dead-drop to icon files on GitHub) and that CISA / Symantec attributed to Diamond Sleet in the X_TRADER (Trading Technologies) and JumpCloud incidents. Generic 'unsigned DLL near signed app' alone is noisy; the high-fidelity gate is t
